# Kubernetes Recipes

> Kubernetes Recipes is a collection of 661+ production-ready Kubernetes guides, tutorials, and troubleshooting recipes by Luca Berton. Covers deployments, networking, security, storage, AI/GPU workloads, Helm, observability, and autoscaling.

- [All Recipes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/): Full recipe index
- [Full LLMs file](https://kubernetes.recipes/llms-full.txt): Complete list of all 661+ recipes with descriptions

## Categories

### Deployments (139 recipes)

- [Browse all deployments recipes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/)
- [How to Implement A/B Testing with Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/ab-testing-kubernetes/): Route traffic between application versions for A/B testing. Use service mesh, ingress, and custom routing rules to validate features with real users.
- [Air-Gapped Image Import for OpenShift Clusters](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/air-gapped-image-import-openshift/): Import container images into disconnected OpenShift clusters. Use podman save/load and internal registries when DNS and TLS block external pulls.
- [Air-Gapped OpenShift with Quay Mirror](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/airgapped-openshift-quay-mirror/): Deploy OpenShift in air-gapped environments with local Quay registry mirror, ImageDigestMirrorSet, and custom CatalogSources.
- [Argo Workflows on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argo-workflows-kubernetes/): deployments
- [ArgoCD App of Apps Pattern](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argocd-app-of-apps-pattern/): Implement the ArgoCD App of Apps pattern to manage multiple applications from a parent Application for cluster bootstrapping.
- ... and 134 more

### Configuration (108 recipes)

- [Browse all configuration recipes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/)
- [How to Manage Kubernetes API Versions and Deprecations](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/api-versions-deprecations/): Handle Kubernetes API version changes and deprecations. Migrate resources to stable APIs and ensure cluster upgrade compatibility.
- [ClusterPolicy MOFED Upgrade Strategy](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/clusterpolicy-mofed-upgrade/): Configure safe MOFED driver upgrade policies in the NVIDIA GPU Operator ClusterPolicy with rolling updates, node draining, and rollback procedures.
- [Fix ConfigMap Changes Not Applied to Pods](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/configmap-hot-reload-troubleshooting/): Debug ConfigMap updates not reflected in running pods. Covers volume mount propagation delays, env var immutability, and sidecar-based reload strategies.
- [How to Manage ConfigMaps and Secrets Effectively](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/configmap-secrets-management/): Master Kubernetes ConfigMaps and Secrets for application configuration. Learn creation methods, mounting strategies, and security best practices.
- [Configure ClusterPolicy kernelModuleType for GPU Operator](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/configure-clusterpolicy-kernel-module-type/): Understand and configure the driver.kernelModuleType field in the NVIDIA GPU Operator ClusterPolicy to choose between auto, open, and proprietary kernel.
- ... and 103 more

### Ai (82 recipes)

- [Browse all ai recipes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/)
- [Batch AI Workloads with Volcano Scheduler on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/ai-batch-processing-volcano/): Schedule and manage batch AI training and inference jobs using Volcano scheduler with gang scheduling, fair-share queues, job plugins, and preemption on.
- [AIPerf Benchmark LLMs on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/aiperf-benchmark-llm-kubernetes/): Deploy NVIDIA AIPerf to benchmark LLM inference performance on Kubernetes. Measure TTFT, ITL, throughput with real-time dashboard and GPU telemetry.
- [AIPerf Concurrency Sweep on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/aiperf-concurrency-sweep-kubernetes/): Run AIPerf concurrency sweeps on Kubernetes to find optimal LLM serving capacity. Automate 1-128 concurrent user benchmarks with batch Jobs.
- [AIPerf Goodput and SLO Benchmarks](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/aiperf-goodput-slo-benchmark/): Measure LLM goodput with AIPerf on Kubernetes. Define SLOs for TTFT and ITL, calculate effective throughput, and benchmark with timeslice analysis.
- [AIPerf Multi-Model Benchmark on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/aiperf-multi-model-benchmark/): Compare multiple LLM models and backends with AIPerf on Kubernetes. Benchmark vLLM vs TGI vs Triton with automated multi-run confidence intervals.
- ... and 77 more

### Troubleshooting (77 recipes)

- [Browse all troubleshooting recipes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/)
- [Fix API Server Timeout and Overload](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/api-server-timeout-troubleshooting/): Debug kubectl timeouts, API server overload, and connection refused errors. Covers etcd latency, webhook timeouts, and rate limiting.
- [Check Bonding and Interface Status for SR-IOV](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/check-bonding-and-interface-status/): Inspect bond membership, interface state, and link aggregation to confirm which NICs can be correctly targeted by SR-IOV network policies on Kubernetes.
- [Fix ConfigMap Too Large Error](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/configmap-too-large-error/): Resolve the 1MB ConfigMap size limit error. Split configs, use Secrets for binary data, mount volumes, or use external stores.
- [Fix CoreDNS Resolution Failures in Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/coredns-troubleshooting/): Debug DNS resolution failures in Kubernetes pods. Covers CoreDNS crashes, NXDOMAIN errors, ndots configuration, and upstream DNS timeouts.
- [CrashLoopBackOff Fix: Kubernetes Troubleshooting](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/crashloopbackoff-troubleshooting/): Fix CrashLoopBackOff in Kubernetes step by step. Debug OOMKilled, missing configs, failed health probes, and image errors causing pod crash loops.
- ... and 72 more

### Security (71 recipes)

- [Browse all security recipes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/security/)
- [How to Create Admission Webhooks](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/security/admission-webhooks/): Build validating and mutating admission webhooks to enforce policies and modify resources. Implement custom admission controllers for Kubernetes.
- [How to Configure Kubernetes API Access Control](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/security/api-access-control/): Set up secure API server access with authentication and authorization. Configure RBAC, API groups, and audit logging for cluster security.
- [How to Manage Kubernetes Certificates with cert-manager](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/security/cert-manager-certificates/): Automate TLS certificate management with cert-manager. Configure issuers, request certificates from Let's Encrypt, and enable automatic renewal.
- [Fix Kubernetes Certificate Expiry Issues](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/security/certificate-expiry-troubleshooting/): Debug and renew expired Kubernetes certificates for API server, kubelet, and etcd. Covers kubeadm cert renewal, OpenShift auto-rotation, and monitoring expiry.
- [Confidential Computing on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/security/confidential-computing-kubernetes/): Deploy confidential containers with encrypted memory using Intel SGX, AMD SEV-SNP, and Kata Containers. Protect data in use from even the cluster admin.
- ... and 66 more

### Networking (71 recipes)

- [Browse all networking recipes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/)
- [Configure SR-IOV agent-config.yaml with Device by Path](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/agent-config-device-by-path/): Use agent-config.yaml to select network devices by PCI path for SR-IOV VF creation, ensuring deterministic NIC targeting across OpenShift nodes.
- [Cilium Service Mesh Without Sidecars](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/cilium-service-mesh-kubernetes/): Deploy Cilium as a sidecarless service mesh on Kubernetes. eBPF-based mTLS, L7 traffic management, and observability without Envoy sidecar overhead.
- [Configure GPUDirect RDMA with the NVIDIA GPU Operator](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/configure-gpudirect-rdma-gpu-operator/): Set up GPUDirect RDMA on Kubernetes using the NVIDIA GPU Operator with either DMA-BUF or legacy nvidia-peermem, including Network Operator integration.
- [Configure SriovNetwork with NVIDIA nv-ipam](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/configure-sriovnetwork-nv-ipam/): Create a SriovNetwork resource that auto-generates a Multus NetworkAttachmentDefinition using nv-ipam for high-performance SR-IOV secondary interfaces.
- [How to Configure Kubernetes Cluster DNS](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/coredns-configuration/): Customize CoreDNS configuration for your cluster. Add custom DNS entries, configure forwarding, and optimize DNS resolution.
- ... and 66 more

### Storage (35 recipes)

- [Browse all storage recipes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/storage/)
- [CNPG Disaster Recovery and Replication](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/storage/cnpg-disaster-recovery/): Set up cross-region PostgreSQL disaster recovery with CloudNativePG using replica clusters, WAL shipping, and automated failover.
- [How to Configure CSI Drivers for Storage](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/storage/csi-drivers-storage/): Install and configure Container Storage Interface (CSI) drivers for cloud and on-premises storage. Set up dynamic provisioning with AWS EBS, GCP PD, and.
- [Distributed fio Storage Benchmarking on Kubernetes and OpenShift](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/storage/distributed-fio-kubernetes-openshift/): Run distributed fio benchmarks on Kubernetes and OpenShift to test storage performance at scale. Covers fio-distributed with k8s Jobs, Red Hat dbench, and CSI throughput validation.
- [How to Configure Dynamic Volume Provisioning](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/storage/dynamic-volume-provisioning/): Set up dynamic volume provisioning in Kubernetes with StorageClasses. Learn to configure provisioners for AWS EBS, GCP PD, Azure Disk, and NFS.
- [Enable GPUDirect Storage on OpenShift](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/storage/enable-gpudirect-storage-openshift/): Configure GPUDirect Storage (GDS) with the NVIDIA GPU Operator on OpenShift, including the Open Kernel Module requirement and nvidia-fs verification.
- ... and 30 more

### Helm (32 recipes)

- [Browse all helm recipes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/)
- [ArgoCD App of Apps with Helm Values](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/argocd-app-of-apps-helm-values/): Use the ArgoCD App of Apps pattern with Helm value overrides per environment, enabling templated Application manifests and DRY multi-environment configurations.
- [Helm before-hook-creation Hook](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/helm-before-hook-creation/): Use Helm before-hook-creation for database migrations and pre-install checks. Complete hook lifecycle, delete policies, and ordering.
- [How to Create and Use Helm Charts](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/helm-chart-basics/): Master Helm, the Kubernetes package manager. Learn to create charts, manage releases, and template your deployments for reusability.
- [How to Create Helm Charts from Scratch](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/helm-chart-creation/): Build custom Helm charts for your applications. Learn chart structure, templates, values, dependencies, and best practices for packaging Kubernetes.
- [Helm Chart Development from Scratch](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/helm-chart-development-guide/): Build production-ready Helm charts with templates, values, helpers, hooks, tests, and CI validation. Complete guide from chart create to publishing.
- ... and 27 more

### Observability (28 recipes)

- [Browse all observability recipes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/observability/)
- [How to Configure Alertmanager for Kubernetes Alerts](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/observability/alertmanager-configuration/): Set up Alertmanager to route, group, and deliver Kubernetes alerts. Learn to configure Slack, PagerDuty, and email notifications.
- [How to Set Up Alertmanager for Prometheus](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/observability/alertmanager-setup/): Configure Alertmanager to route and manage Prometheus alerts. Set up notification channels including Slack, PagerDuty, and email with routing rules.
- [How to Implement Container Logging Patterns](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/observability/container-logging-patterns/): Configure logging for Kubernetes applications. Implement sidecar logging, log aggregation, and structured logging best practices.
- [How to Set Up Container Logging](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/observability/container-logging/): Implement effective logging strategies for Kubernetes containers. Configure log collection, aggregation, and analysis with various logging patterns.
- [How to Implement Distributed Tracing with Jaeger](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/observability/distributed-tracing-jaeger/): Deploy Jaeger for distributed tracing in Kubernetes. Learn to instrument applications, trace requests across services, and identify performance.
- ... and 23 more

### Autoscaling (18 recipes)

- [Browse all autoscaling recipes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/)
- [Kubernetes Cluster Autoscaler Setup Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/cluster-autoscaler-setup/): Configure the Cluster Autoscaler to automatically add and remove nodes based on pod scheduling demands. Covers AWS, GKE, Azure, and bare-metal setups.
- [How to Configure Cluster Autoscaler](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/cluster-autoscaler/): Automatically scale your Kubernetes cluster nodes based on workload demand. Learn to configure Cluster Autoscaler for AWS, GCP, and Azure.
- [HPA Kubernetes: Horizontal Pod Autoscaler](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/horizontal-pod-autoscaler/): Configure HPA in Kubernetes for auto-scaling pods on CPU, memory, and custom metrics. Horizontal Pod Autoscaler examples, thresholds, and best practices.
- [How to Scale Based on Custom Metrics](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/hpa-custom-metrics/): Configure Horizontal Pod Autoscaler with custom and external metrics. Learn to scale on application-specific metrics like queue depth and request latency.
- [Karpenter Node Autoscaling for Kubernetes on AWS](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/karpenter-node-autoscaling-kubernetes/): Deploy Karpenter for fast, flexible node autoscaling on AWS EKS. Configure NodePools, EC2NodeClasses, consolidation, and GPU provisioning with sub-minute node startup.
- ... and 13 more
